extra.message: User input from request.GET.get('p') is directly concatenated into file path via os.path.join(base_path, filename) without proper validation. Attacker can use path traversal sequences to read arbitrary files on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from request.POST.get('first_name', '') is base64 decoded and executed directly via exec(). This allows arbitrary Python code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: User input from request.POST.get('first_name', '') is base64 decoded and executed directly via exec() in Python 2 fallback. This allows arbitrary Python code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: User input from request.GET.get('qs', 'hello') is passed directly to template without escaping. Attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript that will be executed in user's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Path parameter from URL is passed directly to template without escaping. Attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript through the path matching group.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from request.GET.get('qs', 'hello') is passed directly to template without escaping. Attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript that will be executed in user's browser.

extra.message: User profile data is accessed and modified based only on userid parameter from URL without any authorization checks. Any user can access and modify any other user's profile (Insecure Direct Object Reference).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Admin functionality is accessible by checking only request.GET.get('action') == 'admin' without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can access admin page by adding action=admin parameter.

cwe: CWE-284: Improper Access Control Checks or Missing Access Control

extra.message: User input from request.GET.get('url') is passed directly to redirect() without validation. Attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external malicious websites for phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: User input from request.GET.get('fwd') is used to lookup and call arbitrary functions via globals().get(forward). Attacker can call any function in the module's global scope, potentially leading to unauthorized actions.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in source code. This key should be stored in environment variables or secure vaults, not in the codebase.
Django CSRF middleware is commented out, disabling CSRF protection for all forms in the application. This allows attackers to perform unauthorized actions on behalf of authenticated users.
@csrf_exempt decorator disables CSRF protection for csrf_image view. This endpoint is vulnerable to CSRF attacks where attackers can perform unauthorized actions through cross-site requests.
DEBUG mode is enabled (DEBUG = True). In production environments, debug mode exposes sensitive information such as source code, configuration, and environment variables in error pages.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
